title: dryopteris wallichiana subsp. madrasensis
intro: Dryopteris wallichiana subsp. madrasensis, also known as the Madras Indian fern, is a subspecies of the Dryopteris wallichiana plant, a type of fern native to the tropical forests of India. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Dryopteris wallichiana subsp. madrasensis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and act as an antiseptic, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea or infusion, and its extracts are also used in traditional medicine to create remedies for various health issues.
